pain_point_label: No Systematic Grant Research or Foundation Development

matches.match_rationale: The core pain is absence of a grant research pipeline — no one is systematically scanning Philippine foundations, international NGOs, USAID, ADB, or World Bank education programs. A research agent can autonomously crawl donor websites, foundation directories, and bilateral program pages, then synthesize findings into a prioritized grant opportunity list. This directly replaces the missing human capacity for proactive donor discovery.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An autonomous agent that weekly scans 30-50 donor/foundation sources and outputs a ranked grant calendar with deadlines, eligibility fit scores, and funding amounts — delivered to the development officer without manual research.

matches.match_rationale: The pain explicitly names inability to write competitive grant proposals as a capacity gap consuming development officer time. LLMs can draft narrative sections of grant proposals — organizational background, program descriptions, theory of change, budget narratives — grounded in institution-specific context files. This directly addresses the proposal-writing bottleneck without needing a dedicated grant writer.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A grant proposal drafting tool that, given a funder brief and school context document, generates a first-draft narrative (problem statement, proposed activities, expected outcomes) tailored to the specific foundation's priorities and eligibility criteria.

matches.match_rationale: Grant applications require citing institutional data — enrollment figures, program outcomes, financial metrics, past projects — scattered across internal documents. RAG enables a development officer to query 'What evidence do we have of student learning outcomes in STEM?' and get grounded, citation-backed answers drawn from school reports, accreditation documents, and program evaluations. This accelerates proposal evidence-gathering significantly.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An internal knowledge base Q&A tool over the institution's historical reports, accreditation filings, and program data, allowing development staff to rapidly retrieve evidence and quotes for grant application narrative sections.

matches.match_rationale: Building and maintaining a grant pipeline requires repetitive multi-step tasks: checking funder deadlines, updating opportunity status, flagging new eligibility windows, generating reminders. A task automation agent can manage the pipeline workflow — ingesting new grants found by the research agent, routing to approval, tracking submission status, and following up on outcomes — replacing manual spreadsheet management.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A grant pipeline management agent that tracks each opportunity through stages (identified → qualified → drafted → submitted → outcome), sends deadline alerts, and updates a shared dashboard — functioning as an AI-powered development office CRM for grants.
